prompt:
Take the FIRST provided reference image (the currently approved step 17 — hand from upper-left lowering the stubby pill-bottle protector tube into the DH-branded zip-seal specimen bag with QR code) and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY in EVERY element — same hand pose, same bag silhouette and proportions, same parted zip seal, same DIALED HEALTH bag-front logo, same QR code, SAME EXACT TUBE PROPORTIONS (stubby narrow pill-bottle, height ≈ 1.5x diameter, body slightly taller than cap — do NOT make the tube slimmer, taller, or wider), same overall composition, same line-art style on pure white background, same line weight.

THE ONLY MODIFICATION — fix the text on the protector tube. The current rendering has the words swapped: HEALTH appears on the BLACK CAP (top section) and DIALED appears on the WHITE BODY (bottom section), so reading top-down on the upright tube produces 'HEALTH DIALED' which is WRONG. Flip the text 180° so the labels are swapped to their correct positions, producing DIALED HEALTH reading top-down:

  • BLACK CAP (top section, pinched by the hand above the zip seal): the word 'DIALED' (not HEALTH). Rendered as a HORIZONTAL band of upright capital letters reading LEFT-TO-RIGHT across the front face of the cap — D, I, A, L, E, D. Each letter is in its NORMAL UPRIGHT ORIENTATION (horizontal baseline, NOT rotated, NOT sideways, NOT vertical). Bold sans-serif. OUTLINED line-art letters (thin WHITE outline strokes against the solid-black cap fill, letter interior remains black — line-art embossed treatment, NOT solid white filled blocks).

  • WHITE BODY (bottom section, lower portion visible faintly through the translucent bag): the word 'HEALTH' (not DIALED). Rendered as a HORIZONTAL band of upright capital letters reading LEFT-TO-RIGHT across the front face of the body — H, E, A, L, T, H. Each letter upright, NOT rotated. Bold sans-serif. OUTLINED line-art letters (thin BLACK outline strokes against the white body fill, letter interior remains white — line-art embossed treatment, NOT solid black filled blocks). Since the body is inside the translucent bag, the HEALTH band is drawn muted/faint to show through the plastic but the word remains clearly identifiable as HEALTH.

  • Top-to-bottom reading of the upright tube: BLACK CAP showing DIALED → WHITE BODY showing HEALTH = 'DIALED HEALTH' top-down.

  • Both words must be CORRECTLY SPELLED and LEGIBLE from the viewer's straight-on angle — no mirroring, no upside-down letters, no scrambling. D-I-A-L-E-D on the cap and H-E-A-L-T-H on the body, both as horizontal bands.

The key change: the word on the BLACK CAP changes from 'HEALTH' to 'DIALED', and the word on the WHITE BODY changes from 'DIALED' to 'HEALTH'. The two labels swap positions. Visually this looks like a 180° flip of the text on the tube.

Do NOT modify anything else — same hand, same bag, same zip seal, same bag-front DH logo, same QR code, same tube proportions (the stubby v4 silhouette must be preserved), same composition, same line weight, same white background, same line-art aesthetic. The ONLY changes vs the reference are: (1) the word on the black cap is now DIALED (was HEALTH), (2) the word on the white body is now HEALTH (was DIALED), (3) both rendered as horizontal bands of outlined line-art capital letters.

No frame, no border, no other labels, no numbers, no arrows.
